step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{A} Based on multiple sources indicating that child mortality is primarily tied to conditions affecting children under 5 years of age — specifically highlighting pneumonia, diarrhea, neonatal sepsis, and vitamin A deficiency — the standard definition of child mortality includes those aged 0–5 years. While some specific diseases affect younger infants (<2 years), the broad epidemiological scope consistently refers to the 0–5 year age group as defining "childhood" mortality.
